## Shafiq Ninaba Portfolio
[Recent project: **ask-the-docs**](https://shafiqninaba.com/work/ask-the-docs)

# Engineering intelligence, one pipeline at a time.

I'm Shafiq, an AI Engineer crafting scalable ML systems — from computer vision to agentic systems. I build end-to-end solutions that ship.

In my free time, I either boulder or work on my personal projects. You can find them [here](https://shafiqninaba.com/work)!

<|firecrawl-page-4-lllmstxt|>
## Ask the Docs
[Projects](https://shafiqninaba.com/work)

# Ask the Docs: Agentic RAG Chatbot for Documentation

![image](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fask-the-docs%2Faskthedocs-cover.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Favatar.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

April 13, 2025

## Overview

**Ask the Docs** is an agentic RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) chatbot built to help users query the documentation of any website they provide. It uses a custom LangGraph agent to determine the best information source—either a local vector store or live web search—and provides answers along with source citations.It’s especially useful for devs working with APIs or frameworks who want fast, contextual answers to documentation questions.

* * *

## Features

- 🔎 **Website Crawling**: Uses a Firecrawl service to crawl technical documentation and clean the HTML content.
- 🧠 **Agentic RAG Pipeline**: LangGraph agents decide whether to retrieve from the vector DB, rewrite the query, or fall back to live search.
- 💬 **Interactive Chat UI**: Built with Streamlit, showing real-time updates, source documents, and streaming answers.
- 🌍 **Web Search Fallback**: If no relevant documents are found, it queries Tavily for up-to-date info.
- ⚙️ **Real-Time WebSocket Integration**: The crawler streams updates (documents, errors, and completion) directly to the frontend.
- 🧩 **Multi-node Graph**: Includes document grader, rewriter, and context assembler within LangGraph.

* * *

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Tools Used |
| --- | --- |
| **Frontend** | Streamlit, WebSockets |
| **Backend** | FastAPI, LangGraph, LangChain, Tavily API |
| **Model** | OpenAI GPT-4o-mini |
| **Storage** | Qdrant (vector database) |
| **Crawler** | Firecrawl (self-hosted on DigitalOcean) |
| **Infra** | Azure Container Apps, Docker, GitHub Actions |

* * *

## Architecture

![Architecture](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fask-the-docs%2Fsoftware-architecture.png&w=3840&q=75)

1. **User inputs a website URL.**
2. **Firecrawl** crawls and extracts the HTML.
3. Content is **chunked**, embedded, and stored in **Qdrant**.
4. User can now ask questions.
5. A **LangGraph agent**:

   - Searches Qdrant for relevant chunks.
   - Grades the chunks.
   - Optionally rewrites the query.
   - Falls back to Tavily if vector search is unhelpful.
6. Final answer is generated using **GPT-4o-mini** with sources.

* * *

## Learnings

- 🧠 Learned how to construct **LangGraph agents** with multiple toolchains.
- 🔄 Built a **full crawling → embedding → querying pipeline**.
- 💬 Integrated **WebSocket** communication between Streamlit and FastAPI.
- 🔍 Tuned **vector search** performance using document grading and query rewriting.
- ☁️ Containerized the full app with **Azure** and **DigitalOcean** hybrid hosting.

* * *

## Screenshots

### Crawling Progress

![Firecrawl stream](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fask-the-docs%2Ffirecrawler.gif&w=3840&q=75)
This gif shows the document crawling and indexing functionality in action. When a user inputs a URL, the Firecrawl web crawling service that is self-hosted on DigitalOcean efficiently traverses through documentation sources, extracting relevant content, and processes it for the vector database. This automated process ensures that all documentation is properly indexed and ready for semantic search and retrieval.

### Chatbot in Action

![Chatbot in action](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fask-the-docs%2Fchatbot.gif&w=3840&q=75)
The chatbot interaction demonstrates the agentic RAG pipeline in practice. Users can ask complex questions about the documentation, and the system intelligently processes these queries, retrieves relevant information from the vector store, and generates comprehensive, accurate responses. Note how the system handles follow-up questions and maintains context throughout the conversation.

### Web Search Fallback

![Web search fallback](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fask-the-docs%2Fwebsearch.gif&w=3840&q=75)
When information isn't available in the indexed documentation, the system seamlessly falls back to web search capabilities. This demonstration shows how the application leverages Tavily web search to supplement its knowledge base, ensuring users receive helpful responses even for queries that go beyond the scope of the ingested documentation.

* * *

## Source Code

[github.com/shafiqninaba/ask-the-docs](https://github.com/shafiqninaba/ask-the-docs)

<|firecrawl-page-5-lllmstxt|>
## CNN Masked Facial Recognition
[Projects](https://shafiqninaba.com/work)

# Facial Recognition with Accessories using CNN

![image](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Ffyp%2Finference.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Favatar.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

February 26, 2024

## Overview

This project is a facial recognition system designed to identify individuals with or without facial accessories such as surgical masks. It was developed as part of my Final Year Project at NTU, combining deep learning, computer vision, and containerized deployment practices. The system uses Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) trained on the Yale Face Database augmented with masked faces using the MaskTheFace tool. The entire application runs within Docker containers for consistent cross-environment deployment.

## Key Features

- **Real-Time Recognition**: A web-based interface allows users to upload images and get recognition results, even with occluded faces.
- **Multi-Container Architecture**: Built with Docker Compose, separating concerns into backend (CNN model), frontend (Flask UI), and database (MySQL).
- **Model Training Pipeline**: Automatically retrains the CNN model when a new identity is uploaded.
- **Mask Augmentation**: Uses MaskTheFace to simulate masked faces in training data for robustness.
- **Interactive Upload and Demo**: Web interface for uploading face images and testing recognition outcomes live.
- **REST API**: The backend exposes a RESTful API to serve predictions and interact with the database.

## Technologies Used

- **TensorFlow & Keras**: For building and training the CNN.
- **Flask**: Backend web server for API and frontend rendering.
- **MySQL**: Stores encoded face data for model training.
- **Docker & Docker Compose**: For packaging each component into isolated, reproducible containers.
- **JavaScript, HTML/CSS**: Used to build the frontend interface.

## Challenges and Learnings

One of the main challenges was enabling accurate face recognition in the presence of occlusions, particularly masks. This was addressed by data augmentation using MaskTheFace, which significantly improved recognition robustness. Another major hurdle was ensuring seamless inter-container communication while maintaining modularity. Learning to configure and optimize Docker Compose services taught me a lot about scalable infrastructure.I also experimented with model tuning and regularization to avoid overfitting. Integrating real-time model retraining with live uploads pushed me to design efficient workflows and memory management strategies in Python.

## Outcome

The project successfully demonstrates how facial recognition systems can adapt to real-world conditions like occlusions. It is fully containerized and easy to deploy on any system with Docker, making it accessible for demonstrations and further development. This project not only improved my skills in deep learning and software deployment but also served as a launchpad for my continued work in AI applications and MLOps.

<|firecrawl-page-6-lllmstxt|>
## Construction Progress Tracking
[Projects](https://shafiqninaba.com/work)

# Construction Progress Tracking with Deep Learning on 360° Imagery

![image](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fconstruction-tracking%2Fcover.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fconstruction-tracking%2Fmanager.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fconstruction-tracking%2Fmanager.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fconstruction-tracking%2Fsoftware-engineer.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fconstruction-tracking%2Fsoftware-engineer.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fconstruction-tracking%2Fsoftware-engineer.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Favatar.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

March 4, 2025

## Overview

To address inefficiencies in construction project handovers and delays in payment due to manual construction progress tracking, we developed an end-to-end deep learning pipeline capable of performing panoptic segmentation on 360° imagery captured at construction sites.

## Key Features

- **Panoptic Segmentation with Deep Learning**: Utilized models like PanopticFPN to segment and identify construction elements (e.g., doors, walls, windows) across 88 annotated classes in equirectangular projection (ERP) images.
- **360° Image Support**: Adapted models to work with equirectangular projections, resolving common ERP challenges like edge discontinuity and distortion near poles.
- **Custom Dataset**: Worked with a dataset of 3,002 ERP images from 7 locations, annotated using CVAT with XML format for training.
- **Extensible Software Architecture**: Applied design patterns (e.g., Strategy Pattern) for maintainability and future extensibility of the pipeline.
- **Performance Evaluation and Metrics**: Added custom IoU metrics and improved class-wise training data coverage.

## Technologies Used

- **PyTorch & Detectron2**: For building and training deep learning models.
- **FiftyOne & CVAT**: For dataset analysis and annotation handling.
- **Python Multiprocessing**: To efficiently preprocess and handle large image sets.
- **CI/CD Pipeline**: Automated testing, deployment of models and documentation.
- **Docker**: For containerized development and reproducibility.

## Challenges and Learnings

- **Inconsistent Annotations**: Required exploratory data analysis and tooling like FiftyOne to identify and fix label issues.
- **Poor Image Quality**: Preprocessing steps were developed to improve image usability by addressing blur, glare, and exposure problems.
- **Edge Discontinuity in ERP**: Built a custom post-processing step to smooth segmentation across the image edges.
- **Model Adaptation**: PanopticFPN needed modifications to accept ERP images and accommodate overlapping polygons in our dataset format.
- **Class Imbalance & Sparse Labels**: Addressed by analyzing location and class distributions and enhancing training data coverage.

## Outcome

The final solution successfully automated construction progress monitoring with high accuracy, significantly reducing manual reporting time. It provided more reliable and consistent data for stakeholders, enhancing project transparency and accelerating payment approvals. The extensible design ensures the system can evolve with new data and use cases, making it a valuable asset for future construction tech applications.

<|firecrawl-page-7-lllmstxt|>
## GovSeek RAG Chatbot
[Projects](https://shafiqninaba.com/work)

# GovSeek: RAG Chatbot for Government Services

![image](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fgov-seek%2Fgovseek-cover.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Favatar.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

March 5, 2025

## Overview

GovSeek is a proof-of-concept chatbot that helps users access accurate and contextual information from Singapore's [government websites](https://www.gov.sg/trusted-sites) using a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline. It combines real-time web scraping, vector embeddings, and OpenAI's GPT models to provide answers grounded in public domain sources.

## Key Features

- **End-to-end Data Pipeline**: Custom web scraper crawls government websites, extracts clean text content, and generates embeddings using OpenAI’s Batch API.
- **Vector Store Integration**: Embeddings are stored in Qdrant for fast and scalable similarity search.
- **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)**: Combines document retrieval and GPT-4o-mini to answer user queries with grounded, up-to-date context.
- **Streamlit Interface**: Users interact with the chatbot via a password-protected frontend deployed on Heroku.
- **Stateful Conversations**: Maintains memory of chat history to deliver contextually rich and coherent responses.

## Technologies Used

- **Python**: Main programming language for backend logic and data pipeline.
- **LangChain + LangGraph**: For constructing and managing the RAG pipeline.
- **OpenAI API**: GPT-4o-mini for generating answers and embeddings.
- **Qdrant**: Vector database for fast document retrieval.
- **Streamlit**: Rapid development of the chatbot interface.
- **Heroku + Docker**: Deployment and containerization of the full-stack app.

## Challenges and Learnings

A significant challenge was efficiently handling large-scale embedding generation without exceeding token or cost limits. The solution involved batching documents through OpenAI’s Batch API and managing retries and failures gracefully.Building a flexible yet production-ready RAG system also involved learning the nuances of vector databases and optimizing prompt construction for factual accuracy. Coordinating scraping, embedding, storage, and retrieval in a clean pipeline provided valuable insight into full-stack LLM systems.

## Outcome

GovSeek successfully demonstrated how a focused RAG architecture could serve citizens with accurate, transparent, and context-rich information. While still a prototype, the system lays the groundwork for future use cases in public service chatbots, knowledge management, and enterprise LLM adoption.The project also helped improve my understanding of scalable ML infrastructure, vector search, and real-world deployment of LLM systems — all key skills for modern AI engineers.

<|firecrawl-page-8-lllmstxt|>

# Shafiq Ninaba

AI Engineer

[GitHub](https://github.com/shafiqninaba) [github](https://github.com/shafiqninaba) [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shafiq-ninaba/) [linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shafiq-ninaba/) [Email](mailto:shafiqninaba@gmail.com) [email](mailto:shafiqninaba@gmail.com)

Shafiq Ninaba is a Singapore-based AI/ML Engineer with a background in Electrical & Electronic Engineering. He builds end-to-end machine learning systems that span computer vision, data engineering, and LLM-powered agentic workflows. With hands-on experience deploying real-world AI solutions, he enjoys building robust, scalable pipelines that go beyond the notebook.

## Work Experience

AI SingaporeJun 2024 – Mar 2025

Associate AI Engineer

- Delivered an MVP for a $360,000 AI project in the construction industry in just 7 months.
- Designed and deployed a full ML pipeline for 360° panoptic segmentation using CNN-based models trained on 3,000+ images with 100+ classes, achieving 95% mean accuracy.
- Built a FastAPI backend to serve models with inference speeds 50% faster than required.
- Developed CI/CD pipelines with test coverage and documentation for maintainability and handover.

PSA Corporation LtdJan 2023 – Jul 2023

Machine Learning Intern (Smart Systems & Solutions)

- Engineered features from crane sensor data to improve predictive maintenance model accuracy.
- Used DataRobot and Python for unsupervised anomaly detection via API integration.
- Streamlined ETL pipelines and automated reporting workflows with Python and SQL, improving efficiency by 90%.

## Studies

Nanyang Technological University (NTU) Aug 2020 – May 2024

Bachelor of Engineering (Electrical and Electronic Engineering), Honours (Distinction).

Specialized in Info-Communications Engineering with modules like Machine Learning Design & Application, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Vision, and Web Application Design.

## Technical skills

Machine Learning & AISkilled in building and deploying ML models with PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, and MLFlow. Experienced with LangChain, LangGraph, and OpenAI APIs for building agentic systems.

Backend & InfraProficient with FastAPI, Flask, Docker, Azure, AWS, Google Cloud Platform, and CI/CD tools for production-grade ML systems.

Data & VisualizationExperienced with Pandas, NumPy, SQL, Apache Hive, and PySpark. Used Plotly and custom dashboards for model insights and data storytelling.

Web DevelopmentBuilt interactive applications with Streamlit, HTML/CSS/JS, and deployed full-stack solutions on Heroku and DigitalOcean.

## SneakerHive E-Commerce App
[Projects](https://shafiqninaba.com/work)

# SneakerHive: A Web App for Sneaker Enthusiasts

![image](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fsneakerhive%2Fbanner.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Favatar.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

![Avatar](https://shafiqninaba.com/_next/image?url=%2Fimages%2Fprojects%2Fconstruction-tracking%2Fsoftware-engineer.png&w=3840&q=75)

November 13, 2023

## Overview

SneakerHive is an e-commerce web application designed for sneaker enthusiasts. It allows users to explore trending footwear, save favorites, track orders, and subscribe to newsletters. The platform also includes admin tools for managing inventory and reviewing product statistics.

## Key Features

- **Trending Footwear Showcase**: Display of popular sneakers directly on the homepage.
- **Product Favorites**: Logged-in users can like sneakers to view them later.
- **Category Browsing**: Products are neatly categorized for faster discovery.
- **Smooth Checkout Process**: Clean and intuitive checkout experience.
- **Newsletter Subscription**: Users can sign up for product news and drops.
- **Account Creation & Order Tracking**: Users can register, log in, and view their orders.
- **Administrative Page**: Admin dashboard to manage sneaker stock and monitor analytics.

## Technologies Used

- **HTML/CSS/JavaScript**: Core frontend implementation.
- **PHP**: Backend logic for handling authentication, inventory, and order flow.
- **MySQL**: Structured relational database to manage user, product, and order data.
- **XAMPP**: Local server environment for development and testing.

## Challenges and Learnings

One challenge was integrating a dynamic admin interface for non-technical users while keeping the backend secure and maintainable. Ensuring smooth data flow between the client UI and MySQL via PHP also required careful structuring. We also encountered hurdles while optimizing the UI for responsiveness across different screen sizes. Through trial and error, we gained hands-on experience in full-stack web development, database management, and building robust user authentication systems.

## Outcome

The project successfully demonstrates a full-stack e-commerce platform tailored for sneaker enthusiasts. It provides a seamless user experience, from browsing to checkout, while also offering powerful admin tools for inventory management. This project not only solidified my skills in web development but also deepened my understanding of the complexities involved in building a full-fledged e-commerce application.
